title: Yacht Rental in Monaco: Costs, Seasons and What to Know for 2026
html:
Monaco, the glittering principality on the French Riviera, offers a unique vantage point from the water. Departing from Port Hercules or Fontvieille Marina, you can expect to pay from €500 for a half-day rental of a small open boat to over €5,000 for a crewed luxury yacht. BoatHire24 connects you directly with local operators to find the perfect match.

Where to set off in Monaco

The main departure points around Monaco are conveniently located within the port system. The four official harbours are Port Hercules, Fontvieille Marina, the Yacht Club de Monaco pontoons and the smaller fishermen's harbour at La Condamine. For a longer cruise, you can head east into Italian waters, reaching Ventimiglia in about 45 minutes at cruising speed, or west towards Cap d'Ail and Saint-Jean-Cap-Ferrat. Many rental skippers offer FVR (Free des Ventes) trips that include a stop at the Roquebrune-Cap-Martin bay or the Larvotto beach anchorage. These destinations are part of the beauty of the Côte d'Azur. You can rent a boat in a few minutes.

Which boat to rent

When you rent a boat in Monaco, the choice of vessel depends on your group size and desired experience. Here are four common options available on the platform. One option worth a look is the QuickSilver Activ 605 Open - 150 HP for hire.

  1. Open speedboat (e.g. QuickSilver Activ 605) Ideal for small groups of up to 4 guests, from €400 for a half-day. You can view the QuickSilver Activ 605 Open - 150 HP for hire on BoatHire24 to see a typical example.
  2. Day cruiser (e.g. Azimut 42) A comfortable cockpit boat with a small cabin, seating 6-8 guests, from €1,200 for a full day.
  3. Motor catamaran (e.g. Lagoon 40) Stable and spacious, good for 8-10 guests, from €1,800 for a full day.
  4. Crewed luxury yacht (e.g. Sunseeker 62) Fully crewed with a captain and stewardess, accommodating up to 12 guests, from €4,500 for a full day.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Monaco?

Prices start at around €400 for a half-day open boat and rise to over €5,000 for a crewed luxury yacht. The actual cost depends on the vessel size, engine power, season and whether you hire a skipper. Below is a typical cost breakdown.

  • Shared tour: €90 per person for a 2-hour trip, maximum 12 guests.
  • Small motorboat half-day: €400-€600 for 4-6 guests, without skipper.
  • Catamaran full-day: €1,500-€2,500 for 8-10 guests, including skipper.
  • Crewed yacht full-day: €3,000-€6,000 for up to 12 guests, with captain and crew.

Fuel is usually charged extra, and you should budget around €80 for a half-day on a small motorboat. You can compare verified boat listings to ensure you get the best value. You can also see more at check availability.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Monaco?

In Monaco, a boating licence is required for private motorboat rental above a certain power threshold, but most rental operators include a skipper. For rental without a skipper, you typically need an ICC (International Certificate of Competence) or equivalent national permit. The operator confirms exact requirements during booking. The high season runs from June through September, when the Mistral wind occasionally picks up in the afternoon, so be prepared for choppy conditions. To see how booking works, visit how boat rental works.

The best anchorages near Monaco are at Cap d'Ail and the Baie des Milliardaires. Arrive early in the summer to secure a spot as the mooring buoys fill up by 10am.

At a glance

  • Price band: €400 to €6,000 per half-day or full-day rental.
  • Peak months: June, July, August, September.
  • Trip length: 2-hour shared tours up to 8-hour full-day charters.
  • Guest capacity: 4 to 12 passengers.
  • Currency: Euro (EUR).

Practical booking tips

It is wise to book at least one week in advance during the high season. The platform provides transparent pricing, customer reviews and secure payment. To understand the process fully, you can read how boat rental works on the BoatHire24 website. Taking a few minutes to check the terms saves surprises later.

Questions about boat rental in Monaco

Can I rent a boat without a skipper in Monaco?

Yes, many boats can be rented bareboat if you hold a valid boating licence, but the most popular charters include a skipper for convenience and safety.

What is the best time of day to rent a boat in Monaco?

Morning departures around 9am allow you to enjoy calm seas and return before the midday sun, while afternoon trips are better for sunset views.

Are there any hidden fees when renting a boat in Monaco?

Apart from the rental fee, you may be charged for fuel, port entrance fees and a refundable security deposit, all clearly stated in the listing details.

Can I rent a boat for a day trip to Italy from Monaco?

Yes, it is a short 6 nautical mile crossing to the Italian border at Ventimiglia, and many boats are licensed for cross-border travel with prior arrangement.

The principality is a premier destination for blue-water adventures. Whether you are planning a family outing or a corporate event, finding the right craft is straightforward on the platform. You can check availability and compare deals across hundreds of listings. If you own a boat in Monaco and would like to generate extra income, you can list it on BoatHire24 to reach a global audience of renters.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Monaco and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
